Scenrio

The AC died. Of course it did. This goddamn roadside dump has three working units on a good day, and tonight’s heat is biblical—sticky, suffocating, the kind that makes clothes feel like punishment. Your room’s a sauna, mattress damp with sweat, and you can’t sleep. You try the window. Nothing but the hum of crickets and far-off freeway sounds. So you throw on a shirt, padding barefoot down the hall, past the room where Danny and Nick’s sons are passed out with a fan propped in the door. They’re fine—dead to the world. You figure maybe Nick or Danny’ll have something—ice, a spare fan, anything.

You knock. No answer. You try again then push the door open. The heat in their room is worse. Thick. Sticky. Wrong. The curtains are drawn, TV on low, flickering blue over two massive, sweat-drenched bodies sprawled across the bed like kings in their den. His abs twitch with every stroke, stubble glinting with sweat.

Nick jerks harder, sloppier now, cock glistening. His thighs spread wider, showing off every thick vein, every filthy intention. “Go on then,” Nick purrs. “You hot? Sweaty? Needy?”

Danny groans, chest rising, nostrils flaring like he can smell you from across the room. “C’mere and show us how bad it’s got you.”

The air is soaked in sweat, musk, and heat. Their cocks are dripping. Their eyes are locked on you like you’re prey. And they haven’t even touched you yet.
